purpose: Typed discourse-marker typology + move-sequence bigrams (what KIND of scaffolding the writer uses, not just how much), PLUS — additive — the writer's EXPLICIT PDTB top-level discourse-relation mix (Comparison / Contingency / Expansion / Temporal) as a descriptive distribution: per-1k densities, fractions, and relation entropy. The relation layer is an explicit-connective PROXY (arXiv:2307.03378): it maps only explicitly-signalled connectives to their PDTB majority top-level class via a static, corpus-independent lexicon; it does not extract argument spans, does not disambiguate polysemous connectives per-occurrence (ambiguous_connective_fraction reports their share), and does not see the implicit-relation layer the trained classifier of arXiv:2307.03378 targets (that is the gated model-CPU M2 seam). NO VERDICT: every leaf is a VALUE (count / density / fraction / entropy); the relation distribution ships uncalibrated (calibration_status: uncalibrated) with no band. Consumed by confounder_audit.py as differentiating evidence; the differential diagnosis of cause is that audit's job, never this layer's.
use_when: Differentiating institutional/legal prose (Contingency-dense) from narrative (Temporal-dense) as confounder evidence
Profiling a writer's explicit discourse-relation mix alongside the marker typology
You want what KIND of scaffolding/relation the writer uses, not just how much
do_not_use_when: You need an AI-provenance verdict or a writing-quality judgment
You need implicit (unsignalled) relation typing — that is the gated M2 model (arXiv:2307.03378)
Non-English text (the connective lexicon is English, case-insensitive regex)

compute.cost_note: stdlib regex (re / collections.Counter / math); no API / model costs.

examples.description: Typed discourse markers + explicit PDTB relation distribution
With a self-baseline corpus (marker + relation z-scores)

references: Side-by-side Transformers for Implicit Discourse Relation Classification (PDTB-3), arXiv:2307.03378: https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.03378
